perturbation.original_text: c) Excite will in "good faith" ensure Client that the above mentioned banners and promotional placements will be more prominently presented than any other "competitive retailer's" banners or promotional placements for the term of the Agreement. For the purposes of this Agreement, a "competitive retailer" means an on-line department store comparable to Bloomingdale's, Macy's, Burdine's, Shopping.com, Chef's Catalog or iQVC.
perturbation.changed_text: c) Excite will use commercially reasonable efforts to display Client's banners and promotional placements. The definition of 'competitive retailer' is left to Excite's sole discretion.
perturbation.explanation: By altering the definition of "competitive retailer" and giving Excite sole discretion over it, the original intention of prominent placement compared to specific competitors becomes vague. This change removes the objective standard for what constitutes a 'competitive retailer' and replaces it with Excite's subjective interpretation, creating a contradiction in enforcing the original intent of prominent placement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: c) Excite will use commercially reasonable efforts to display Client's banners and promotional placements. The definition of 'competitive retailer' is left to Excite's sole discretion. d) Excite will provide Client with monthly reports substantiating the number of impressions of Client's advertising banners and promotional placements displayed on the Excite Network and the resulting number of clickthroughs to the Client's site. At the time that Excite makes audited impression reports available to its advertisers, Client will receive audited impression reports.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly alters the definition of 'competitive retailer' from a specific list to Excite's sole discretion. This contradicts the underlying expectation of the original agreement, where Excite was to ensure the client's banners were more prominently displayed than banners of comparable retailers. The original text, by specifying particular retailers, set a measurable standard. The changed text provides no such standard, undermining the client's guarantee of prominent placement and making the standard entirely subjective. Because the contradicted text also reflects the changed text, it shows the explicit contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: i) Client will not use User Data to directly or indirectly solicit any Excite users either individually or in the aggregate during the term of this Agreement and for a period of twelve (12) months following the expiration or termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: i) Client will endeavor not to use User Data to directly solicit any Excite users during the term of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'will not' to 'will endeavor not to' and removing the twelve-month restriction following the agreement's expiration, the obligation to not solicit Excite users after the agreement is weakened. This creates an in-text contradiction because the previous clause implies a strict prohibition, while the modified clause suggests a less stringent requirement. This undermines the protection of Excite's user base.
perturbation.contradicted_text: h) Client will provide to Excite all User Data collected by Client within thirty (30) days following the end of each calendar month during the term of this Agreement in a mutually-determined electronic format. i) Client will endeavor not to use User Data to directly solicit any Excite users during the term of this Agreement. j) Neither party will sell, disclose, transfer or rent any User Data which could reasonably be used to identify a specific named individual ("Individual Data") to any third party nor will either party use Individual Data on behalf of any third party without the express permission of the individual user.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited solicitation for 12 months after termination. The changed text weakens this obligation by changing 'will not' to 'will endeavor not to' and removing the post-termination restriction. This directly contradicts the implied intent of clause h) in the contradicted text, which outlines the provision of user data, and renders uncertain the purpose of providing that data if the client's obligation to not solicit is weakened. Furthermore, it undermines the purpose of clause j) which limits the use of Individual Data, because Client can now solicit aggregate, non-individually-identifiable data after the contract terminates. The modified text creates a strong inconsistency that undermines the user base protection intended by the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The parties agree that any breach of either of the parties' obligations regarding trademarks, service marks or trade names, confidentiality and/or User Data would result in irreparable injury for which there is no adequate remedy at law. Therefore, in the event of any breach or threatened breach of a party's obligations regarding trademarks, service marks or trade names or confidentiality, the aggrieved party will be entitled to seek equitable relief in addition to its other available legal remedies in a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: The parties acknowledge that potential breaches of confidentiality may occur. Therefore, in the event of a confidentiality breach, the aggrieved party will be entitled to seek monetary damages.
perturbation.explanation: By removing User Data and obligations regarding trademarks, service marks or trade names from the clause concerning irreparable injury and equitable relief, and limiting the available remedy to monetary damages, the clause contradicts the initial stronger stance on protecting those specific assets. The original clause emphasized irreparable harm, justifying equitable relief, while the modified clause only acknowledges potential breaches and focuses on monetary compensation, creating a conflict in the level of protection afforded to those assets.
perturbation.contradicted_text: a) The parties acknowledge that potential breaches of confidentiality may occur. Therefore, in the event of a confidentiality breach, the aggrieved party will be entitled to seek monetary damages. b) In the event of disputes between the parties arising from or concerning in any manner the subject matter of this Agreement, other than disputes arising from or concerning trademarks, service marks or trade names, confidentiality and/or User Data, the parties will first attempt to resolve the dispute(s) through good faith negotiation. In the event that the dispute(s) cannot be resolved through good faith negotiation, the parties will refer the dispute(s) to a mutually acceptable mediator.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the remedy for a confidentiality breach to monetary damages, while the original text and the contradicted text specify that breaches related to trademarks, service marks, trade names, confidentiality, and User Data would result in irreparable injury, justifying equitable relief. This creates a contradiction because the modified text now conflicts with the provision emphasizing the unique harm and remedies associated with those assets, creating a conflict on the level of protection.
